source_excerpt:
The Office of
5 Chief Counsel Prosecution Division works with its
6 investigative (BEI) and compliance arms (PCO), who
7 receive complaints, investigate allegations, and
8 enforce compliance with the acts and regulations that
9 govern the boards and commissions. She stated the
10 Prosecution Division consists of five senior
11 prosecuting attorneys and 34 prosecutors.
12 Ms. Walter stated the prosecution process always
13 begins with a complaint either from the public or by
14 Board referral. She reported, in both 2024 and 2025,
15 the department received approximately 22,800 total
16 complaints for all boards. Ms. Walter provided an
17 overview of the investigative process in which the
18 complaint moves through several levels of review. She
19 noted the primary role of prosecution at Board
20 meetings is to present Consent Agreements to the
21 Board.
commentary: NEGATIVE example. This is a procedural report describing the Prosecution Division's general role, staffing, and complaint process - it names no disciplinary outcome against any licensee, so correct_facts is empty. The 'approximately 22,800 total complaints for all boards' figure is a statewide workload statistic, NOT a disciplinary-action count for this board's meeting; treating it as a count would fabricate actions.
